2603.18968.title: Teleological Inference in Structural Causal Models via Intentional Interventions
2603.18968.paper_summary: Structural causal models (SCMs) were conceived to formulate and answer causal questions. This paper shows that SCMs can also be used to formulate and answer teleological questions, concerning the intentions of a state-aware, goal-directed agent intervening in a causal system. We review limitations of previous approaches to modeling such agents, and then introduce intentional interventions, a new time-agnostic operator that induces a twin SCM we call a structural final model (SFM). SFMs treat observed values as the outcome of intentional interventions and relate them to the counterfactual conditions of those interventions (what would have happened had the agent not intervened). We show how SFMs can be used to empirically detect agents and to discover their intentions.
2603.18968.paper_summary_zh: 結構因果模型（SCMs）是為了制定和回答因果問題而構思的。本文顯示，SCMs 也可以用來制定和回答目的論問題，這些問題涉及一個具有狀態感知和目標導向的代理在因果系統中進行干預的意圖。我們回顧了之前建模此類代理的方法的局限性，然後引入了意圖干預，一種新的時間無關運算符，該運算符引入了我們稱之為結構最終模型（SFM）的雙胞胎 SCM。SFMs 將觀察到的值視為意圖干預的結果，並將其與這些干預的反事實條件相關聯（如果代理沒有干預會發生什麼）。我們展示了如何使用 SFMs 來實證檢測代理並發現其意圖。
2603.18968.author: Dario Compagno et.al.
